name: carddemo
notes: AWS CardDemo: COBOL/CICS/VSAM with JCL batch + CSD-defined transactions. All online navigation is DYNAMIC (EXEC CICS XCTL PROGRAM(CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM)) — zero literal XCTL targets exist, so no tool can produce exact online caller graphs from source alone; completeness entries encode that. COBDATFT/MVSWAIT are in-repo HLASM (app/asm/) called from COBOL — deliberately neutral in gaps (cross-language; reporting them as gaps is honest until HLASM support lands, so neither rewarded nor punished).

queries.evidence: app/cbl/COACTUPC.cbl header: accept and process account update

queries.q: where are credit card transactions posted in batch
queries.evidence: app/jcl/POSTTRAN.jcl runs CBTRN02C (post records from daily transaction file)
app/cbl/CBSTM03A.cbl statement generation, calls CBSTM03B for file I/O
app/cbl/COSGN00C.cbl signon screen; CSD tran CC00
app/cbl/CSUTLDTC.cbl date conversion/validation, called by CORPT00C and COTRN02C

completeness.why: reached only via dynamic XCTL PROGRAM(CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM); its own dispatch is dynamic too (menu router)
invoked via CSD transaction CAUP and dynamic XCTL — no literal caller exists in source
dynamic XCTL at line ~ (CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM); entry via CSD tran CC00
called only via literal CALL 'CBSTM03B' from CBSTM03A (batch, fully static)
called only via literal CALL 'CSUTLDTC' (CORPT00C, COTRN02C)

gaps.evidence: CICS (DFH*), MQ (MQ*), LE (CEE*), DB2 (SQLCA) and z/OS utilities are platform-provided; a gap report naming them as acquisition targets is noise. The repo itself is complete, so must_report_missing is empty.
